message:
Add comprehensive test suite for CircuitStats state machine

This commit adds extensive unit tests for the CircuitStats implementation,
verifying correct behavior of the circuit breaker state machine that
manages worker health and failover decisions.

Test coverage includes:

State machine fundamentals:
- test_circuit_stats_new: Verifies initial state (Closed, 0 failures, 0% error rate)
- test_circuit_state_transitions_are_deterministic: Full lifecycle test from
  Closed -> Open -> HalfOpen -> Closed with proper triggers

Recording and metrics:
- test_circuit_stats_record_success: Consecutive successes tracking
- test_circuit_stats_record_failure: Consecutive failures tracking
- test_circuit_stats_error_rate: Accurate error rate calculation (failures/total)
- test_circuit_stats_success_resets_consecutive_failures: Success clears failure count

State transitions:
- test_circuit_stats_open_transition: Open state sets opened_at timestamp
- test_circuit_stats_half_open_transition: HalfOpen state from Open
- test_circuit_stats_close_transition: Close clears state, resets counters

Threshold logic:
- test_circuit_stats_should_open_consecutive_failures: Opens after N consecutive failures
- test_circuit_stats_should_open_error_rate: Opens when error rate exceeds threshold
  (requires minimum 5 samples to avoid false positives on small sample sizes)
- test_circuit_stats_should_close: Closes after M consecutive successes in HalfOpen

Probing behavior:
- test_circuit_stats_can_probe: Probe only allowed in HalfOpen state
- test_circuit_stats_probe_completion: Probe completion allows next probe
- test_circuit_stats_failure_in_half_open: Failure resets success counter, stays HalfOpen

Window management:
- test_circuit_stats_reset_window: Clears rolling window for fresh error rate calculation

These tests ensure the circuit breaker behaves correctly under all conditions,
preventing cascading failures when workers become unhealthy while allowing
quick recovery when they return to service.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
